stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 10 observations with mean 69.6 % (within-trial site SD = 5.0). trial site B has 20 observations with mean 65.3 % (within-trial site SD = 7.7). trial site C has 15 observations with mean 69.2 % (within-trial site SD = 9.8). trial site D has 15 observations with mean 68.3 % (within-trial site SD = 5.6). trial site E has 15 observations with mean 65.6 % (within-trial site SD = 3.8). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.4 and 8.4 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site D: 68.2 % (shrunk from raw 68.3). Range [68.1, 68.3] as between-trial site SD varies from 3.4 to 8.4. Pooled mean: 67.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 67.3 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=5.6: B = 0.938.
Interval [68.1, 68.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (68.3) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (67.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
